objective: Test whether a physically specified 5 eV ion-source operating point remains near the oaTOF 5 eV reference after the existing collision-free RF quadrupole, without changing geometry, fields or handoff velocity.

input_candidate.distribution_policy: Reuse the official birth-time, position and filled-cone direction distributions; change only the named input energy operating point.

model_changes.future_physical_provider: upstream ion source and extraction optics

n100_evidence.excluded_reason: The fixed-energy generator branch did not consume an energy random quantile, so downstream direction samples were not particle-wise paired.
n100_evidence.decision: PASS for RF-handoff mean-energy matching only. The larger radius and divergence require the existing physical-port and oaTOF downstream test; they do not authorize parameter optimization or Formal source replacement.

physical_port_pulse_evidence.pulse_time_rule: Select one explicit mass_amu and charge_state group, predict finite-wall survivors from actual handoff positions and velocities, then solve the shared time at which their mean x equals the oaTOF ideal-source center.
physical_port_pulse_evidence.timing_rule_scope: Continuous-beam time-slice extraction; no compact-storage or hit-rate requirement.

legacy_14mm_timing.reason: The 14 mm isolation rule pulsed before the active ion centroid reached the oaTOF ideal-source center.

physical_port_pulse_evidence.decision: PASS for state-driven centroid timing of continuous-beam slice extraction. Hit rate is intentionally not a timing gate; this does not claim compact storage, resolution, S1 promotion or Formal integration.
